CAR reporters are getting more popular

Uplink editors editor April 1994
Journalism & NewsroomInvestigative Methods

Several journalism staffing changes occur as reporters move between major newspapers, including Dan Browning's move to the St. Paul Pioneer Press and David Armstrong's transfer to the Boston Globe, while The Hartford Courant expands its computer-assisted reporting program.

Dan Browning (author of last month's food inspection article) has headed north from the St. Louis Post-Dispatch to the St. Paul Pioneer Press. David Armstrong has gone crosstown from the Boston Herald to the Boston Globe and his partner, Alan Levin, took Brant Houston's spot at The Hartford Courant. Actually, The Courant has expanded its CAR program by adding John Moran, a long-time reporter there, as its online reporter.

(This issue's quiz: How many online reporters are there in the country? Find out next issue.) And, of course, Jennifer LaFleur of the San Jose Mercury News joined NICAR March 28.
